finding_freerun_mode: When TSK FREE-RUNS without wrfout to replay (true operational forecast beyond replay coverage), a TSK frozen at t0 is wrong by up to the static-TSK error above. TSK leads T2, so that error far exceeds the 1.5 K T2 RMSE gate. A static TSK is NOT adequate for free-running 24-72h T2.
finding_replay_mode: When TSK is DATA-REPLAYED from wrfout (current divergence_map design), TSK carries the full real diurnal swing -> radiation lower boundary is correct -> static-per-step-replay TSK is ADEQUATE for the M9 validation gate; the diurnal SW driver (this lane) responds to it.
land_vs_water: Ocean (SST) TSK swing is small (thermal inertia); concern is land-only. Canary d02 is ocean-dominated -> reduces but does not eliminate the need on the islands themselves.
question: Is a static/prescribed TSK lower boundary adequate for 24-72h T2, or is prognostic Noah-MP needed?
recommendation_report_not_decide: M9 replay/validation gate: static TSK replay adequate; promote nothing yet. Operational free-running 24-72h: a prognostic skin-temperature budget (minimum) or prognostic Noah-MP IS required; the prescribed-subset noah_mp.py is insufficient as a free lower boundary. Manager + later discriminator decide; threshold = whether the operational product runs beyond wrfout-replay coverage.
